33.reason: Error: p[i] is not allocated before accessing p[i]->n; also i is incremented before second access

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: p[i] is not allocated before accessing p[i]->n; also i is incremented before second access
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: i is incremented before accessing p[i]->n, causing out-of-bounds access. Also, p[i] may not be allocated.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: accessing p[1] before allocation; scanf loop condition and increment logic corrupts memory]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  error: (1) trailing space in scanf format may cause waiting for input; (2) p[i] after i++ is not allocated, leading to segmentation fault
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  p[i] is uninitialized when accessed in scanf, and i++ causes coefficient and exponent to be stored in different struct instances. Trailing space in format string may also cause input hanging.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  scanf does not stop at newline, reads both polynomials into one array; also p[i] is uninitialized when accessing n
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Indexing error and uninitialized pointer: p[i] is used before being allocated when i increments; also, scanf format may not handle input correctly as it expects space after last term which may not exist.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Undefined behavior: p[i] is used before being allocated when i increments. Also, the second %d reads into p[i]->n but p[i] hasn't been malloc'd yet.]

39.reason: error: separation logic assumes polynomial exponents strictly decrease, but if second polynomial has exponent 0 (constant) then break condition never triggers, incorrectly merging polynomials

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Logic to split two polynomials is flawed; assumes exponent increases at boundary, but input is typically descending, leading to incorrect split.
